decision_a.title: Use JWT for API authentication
decision_a.description: Stateless JWT tokens for all API auth

decision_b.title: Use server-side sessions with Redis for API authentication
decision_b.description: Session-based auth with Redis store for session management

test_cases.explanation: Mutually exclusive authentication strategies: stateless JWT vs stateful sessions

decision_a.title: Use PostgreSQL for all data storage
decision_a.description: Single PostgreSQL database for all application data including caching

decision_b.title: Use MongoDB as the primary database
decision_b.description: MongoDB document store for flexible schema and horizontal scaling

test_cases.explanation: Different primary databases cannot coexist for the same data

decision_a.title: Deploy with Docker Compose on VPS
decision_a.description: Self-hosted Docker deployment on dedicated VPS

decision_b.title: Deploy with AWS Lambda serverless
decision_b.description: Serverless deployment using AWS Lambda and API Gateway

test_cases.explanation: Fundamentally different deployment architectures

decision_a.title: Use REST API with JSON
decision_a.description: RESTful API design with JSON request/response bodies

decision_b.title: Use GraphQL as the primary API
decision_b.description: GraphQL API for flexible client queries and reduced over-fetching

test_cases.explanation: REST and GraphQL as primary API are mutually exclusive architectural choices

decision_a.title: Use Tailwind CSS for styling
decision_a.description: Utility-first CSS framework for rapid UI development

decision_b.title: Use CSS Modules for component styling
decision_b.description: Scoped CSS modules for component-level style encapsulation

test_cases.explanation: Different CSS methodologies as primary styling approach

decision_a.title: Use Prisma ORM
decision_a.description: Prisma for type-safe database access with auto-generated client

decision_b.title: Use raw SQL queries only
decision_b.description: Direct SQL queries for maximum control and performance

test_cases.explanation: ORM vs raw SQL are opposing database access strategies

decision_a.title: Single-tenant architecture
decision_a.description: Each customer gets their own database and server instance

decision_b.title: Multi-tenant shared database
decision_b.description: All customers share one database with RLS for isolation

test_cases.explanation: Opposing multi-tenancy strategies

decision_a.title: Monorepo with Turborepo
decision_a.description: All packages in one repo with Turbo for build orchestration

decision_b.title: Separate repos per package
decision_b.description: Each package in its own repository with independent versioning

test_cases.explanation: Monorepo vs polyrepo are mutually exclusive code organization strategies

decision_a.title: Express.js for HTTP server
decision_a.description: Express framework for Node.js HTTP handling

decision_b.title: Hono for HTTP server
decision_b.description: Hono framework for edge-ready HTTP handling

test_cases.explanation: Different HTTP frameworks for the same server

decision_a.title: Jest as test runner
decision_a.description: Jest for all unit and integration tests

decision_b.title: Vitest as test runner
decision_b.description: Vitest for native ESM and TypeScript test support

test_cases.explanation: Different test runners for the same test suite

decision_a.title: Store files in S3
decision_a.description: AWS S3 for all file and asset storage

decision_b.title: Store files on local filesystem
decision_b.description: Local disk storage for uploaded files and assets

test_cases.explanation: Different file storage strategies

decision_a.title: Use WebSockets for all real-time features
decision_a.description: WebSocket connections for live updates and collaboration

decision_b.title: Use Server-Sent Events for real-time
decision_b.description: SSE for one-way real-time server-to-client updates

test_cases.explanation: Different real-time communication protocols as primary choice

decision_a.title: Dark mode as default theme
decision_a.description: Dashboard defaults to dark color scheme

decision_b.title: Light mode as default theme
decision_b.description: Dashboard defaults to light color scheme for readability

test_cases.explanation: Opposing default theme choices

decision_a.title: Offset-based pagination
decision_a.description: Use LIMIT/OFFSET for paginating list endpoints

decision_b.title: Cursor-based pagination
decision_b.description: Use cursor tokens for consistent pagination under concurrent writes

test_cases.explanation: Different pagination strategies for the same endpoints

decision_a.title: Use npm for package management
decision_a.description: npm as the package manager for the project

decision_b.title: Use pnpm for package management
decision_b.description: pnpm for efficient disk usage and strict dependency resolution

test_cases.explanation: Different package managers for the same project

decision_a.title: Allow nullable foreign keys for flexibility
decision_a.description: Foreign keys can be null to support partial data ingestion

decision_b.title: Enforce strict NOT NULL on all foreign keys
decision_b.description: All foreign keys must reference valid records, no nulls allowed

test_cases.explanation: Opposing database schema strictness policies

decision_a.title: Log all request bodies for debugging
decision_a.description: Full request body logging in development and staging

decision_b.title: Never log request bodies containing credentials
decision_b.description: Strip sensitive fields from all log output

test_cases.explanation: Conflicting logging policies regarding sensitive data

decision_a.title: Use argon2 for password hashing
decision_a.description: Argon2id for memory-hard password hashing

decision_b.title: Use bcrypt for password hashing
decision_b.description: bcrypt with cost factor 12 for password hashing

test_cases.explanation: Different password hashing algorithms as primary choice

decision_a.title: React for frontend framework
decision_a.description: React with hooks for the dashboard SPA

decision_b.title: Vue.js for frontend framework
decision_b.description: Vue 3 with Composition API for the dashboard

test_cases.explanation: Different frontend frameworks for the same application

decision_a.title: 60-second JWT token expiry
decision_a.description: Very short-lived tokens refreshed frequently for security

decision_b.title: 24-hour JWT token expiry
decision_b.description: Long-lived tokens for better UX, fewer refresh calls

test_cases.explanation: Conflicting token lifetime policies

decision_a.title: Use TypeScript strict mode
decision_a.description: Enable strict TypeScript checking across all packages

decision_b.title: Use ESLint with TypeScript rules
decision_b.description: ESLint configured with @typescript-eslint for code quality

test_cases.explanation: TypeScript strict mode and ESLint are complementary quality tools

decision_a.title: Use PostgreSQL for data storage
decision_a.description: PostgreSQL as the primary relational database

decision_b.title: Use Redis for caching
decision_b.description: Redis for session caching and rate limit counters

test_cases.explanation: PostgreSQL for storage and Redis for caching serve different purposes

decision_a.title: Deploy with Docker
decision_a.description: Containerized deployment using Docker

decision_b.title: Use GitHub Actions for CI/CD
decision_b.description: Automated testing and deployment via GitHub Actions

test_cases.explanation: Docker deployment and GitHub Actions CI/CD are complementary

decision_a.title: Use React Query for server state
decision_a.description: TanStack Query for API data fetching and caching

decision_b.title: Use Zustand for client state
decision_b.description: Zustand for lightweight client-side state management

test_cases.explanation: Server state (React Query) and client state (Zustand) are different concerns

decision_a.title: Implement RBAC
decision_a.description: Role-based access control with admin, editor, viewer roles

decision_b.title: Implement audit logging
decision_b.description: Log all mutations to an audit trail table

test_cases.explanation: RBAC and audit logging are independent security features

decision_a.title: Run unit tests on every PR
decision_a.description: CI runs the full unit test suite on pull requests

decision_b.title: Run integration tests nightly
decision_b.description: Full integration test suite runs on a nightly schedule

test_cases.explanation: Unit tests on PR and integration tests nightly are complementary CI strategies

decision_a.title: Use Nginx as reverse proxy
decision_a.description: Nginx for TLS termination and static file serving

decision_b.title: Use Cloudflare for CDN and DDoS protection
decision_b.description: Cloudflare in front of origin for caching and protection

test_cases.explanation: Nginx and Cloudflare serve at different layers of the stack

decision_a.title: Use Vitest for testing
decision_a.description: Vitest as the test framework

decision_b.title: Use Testing Library for DOM assertions
decision_b.description: React Testing Library for component behavior testing

test_cases.explanation: Vitest (runner) and Testing Library (assertions) work together

decision_a.title: Use D3.js for graph visualization
decision_a.description: D3 for rendering force-directed decision graphs

decision_b.title: Use React for the dashboard shell
decision_b.description: React for component composition and routing

test_cases.explanation: D3 handles visualization within React components

decision_a.title: Use bcrypt for passwords
decision_a.description: bcrypt hashing for user passwords

decision_b.title: Use SHA-256 for API key hashing
decision_b.description: SHA-256 for hashing API keys before storage

test_cases.explanation: Different hashing for different purposes: passwords vs API keys

decision_a.title: Deploy daily to staging
decision_a.description: Automated daily deployments to staging environment

decision_b.title: Run tests daily in CI
decision_b.description: Full test suite runs daily on a schedule

test_cases.explanation: Daily deploys and daily tests are independent processes in different domains

decision_a.title: Use Zod for API validation
decision_a.description: Zod schemas validate all incoming request bodies

decision_b.title: Use TypeScript strict mode
decision_b.description: Strict TypeScript for compile-time type safety

test_cases.explanation: Runtime validation (Zod) and compile-time types (TS) complement each other

decision_a.title: Use pino for server logging
decision_a.description: Structured JSON logging with pino

decision_b.title: Use Sentry for error tracking
decision_b.description: Sentry for exception monitoring and alerting

test_cases.explanation: Logging and error tracking serve different observability needs

decision_a.title: Implement rate limiting
decision_a.description: Token bucket rate limiter on API endpoints

decision_b.title: Implement request validation
decision_b.description: Validate all request inputs before processing

test_cases.explanation: Rate limiting and validation are independent API protection layers

decision_a.title: Use conventional commits
decision_a.description: Enforce conventional commit message format

decision_b.title: Use semantic versioning
decision_b.description: Follow semver for all package releases

test_cases.explanation: Conventional commits and semver are complementary versioning practices

decision_a.title: Use Node.js 18 LTS
decision_a.description: Node 18 as the runtime for all server packages

decision_b.title: Upgrade to Node.js 22 LTS
decision_b.description: Node 22 for improved performance and native fetch support

test_cases.explanation: Node 22 supersedes Node 18 as the newer LTS version

decision_a.title: Use Express.js v4
decision_a.description: Express 4 for the HTTP server

decision_b.title: Migrate to Hono framework
decision_b.description: Hono for better TypeScript support and edge compatibility

test_cases.explanation: Hono replaces Express as an intentional migration

decision_a.title: Deploy to Heroku
decision_a.description: Heroku for simple PaaS deployment

decision_b.title: Migrate to Docker on VPS
decision_b.description: Self-hosted Docker deployment for cost and control

test_cases.explanation: VPS Docker deployment supersedes Heroku as an intentional migration

decision_a.title: Use Moment.js for dates
decision_a.description: Moment.js for date formatting and manipulation

decision_b.title: Switch to date-fns
decision_b.description: date-fns for tree-shakeable, immutable date utilities

test_cases.explanation: date-fns supersedes Moment.js as a modern replacement

decision_a.title: Use Create React App
decision_a.description: CRA for React project bootstrapping and build

decision_b.title: Migrate to Vite
decision_b.description: Vite for faster development builds and better ESM support

test_cases.explanation: Vite supersedes CRA as the build tool

decision_a.title: 80% code coverage target
decision_a.description: Minimum 80% line coverage enforced in CI

decision_b.title: Increase coverage target to 90%
decision_b.description: Raised coverage bar to 90% as codebase matures

test_cases.explanation: 90% target supersedes the previous 80% target

decision_a.title: Use Prettier with default config
decision_a.description: Prettier for code formatting with default settings

decision_b.title: Customize Prettier to use single quotes and no semicolons
decision_b.description: Updated Prettier config to match team style preferences

test_cases.explanation: Custom config supersedes default config for the same tool

decision_a.title: Use JWT with 1-hour expiry
decision_a.description: Access tokens valid for 1 hour

decision_b.title: Reduce JWT expiry to 15 minutes
decision_b.description: Shortened token lifetime for improved security posture

test_cases.explanation: 15-minute expiry supersedes 1-hour as a security improvement

decision_a.title: Use npm scripts for task running
decision_a.description: package.json scripts for build, test, lint tasks

decision_b.title: Adopt Turborepo for task orchestration
decision_b.description: Turbo for parallel builds and intelligent caching across monorepo

test_cases.explanation: Turborepo supersedes npm scripts for monorepo task management

decision_a.title: Manual database backups weekly
decision_a.description: DBA runs pg_dump manually every Friday

decision_b.title: Automated daily database backups with retention
decision_b.description: Cron job runs pg_dump daily, retains 30 days, uploads to S3

test_cases.explanation: Automated daily backups supersede manual weekly backups

decision_a.title: Use basic console.log for debugging
decision_a.description: Console logging for development debugging

decision_b.title: Implement structured logging with pino
decision_b.description: JSON structured logging for production observability

test_cases.explanation: Structured logging supersedes console.log as the project matures

decision_a.title: SQLite for development database
decision_a.description: SQLite for fast local development without PostgreSQL

decision_b.title: Use Docker PostgreSQL for all environments
decision_b.description: Docker Compose PostgreSQL matches production in all environments

test_cases.explanation: Docker PostgreSQL supersedes SQLite for dev environment parity

decision_a.title: Use Axios for HTTP requests
decision_a.description: Axios as the HTTP client library

decision_b.title: Switch to native fetch API
decision_b.description: Native fetch with thin wrapper, no external dependency

test_cases.explanation: Native fetch supersedes Axios as Node 18+ includes native fetch

decision_a.title: Store secrets in .env files
decision_a.description: Environment variables in .env files for configuration

decision_b.title: Use cloud secret manager for production secrets
decision_b.description: AWS Secrets Manager for production, .env only for local dev

test_cases.explanation: Cloud secret manager supersedes .env files for production environments

decision_a.title: Manual PR reviews by one developer
decision_a.description: Single reviewer required for PR approval

decision_b.title: Require two reviewers including security for sensitive PRs
decision_b.description: Enhanced review policy: 2 approvals, security review for auth/db changes

test_cases.explanation: Enhanced review policy supersedes the simpler single-reviewer policy
